items.title: Malaysia's digital push must uplift all, not just urban groups
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: Malaysia's digital transformation drive risks failure if it does not uplift the majority of the rakyat, as technological progress must go hand in hand with equity, values and access, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. He said while the push towards a digital economy — including artificial intelligence (AI), data centres and advanced technologies — is critical for growth, jobs and competitiveness, its success must ultimately be measured by how widely benefits are shared. Anwar said Malaysia is in a strong position to attract global investments from countries such as the United States, China, India and Germany, but cautioned that this advantage must not translate into gains for only urban or well-connected groups.

items.title: Johor marine police foils attempt to smuggle nearly 700,000 lobster fry to Singapore
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The Johor Region Two Marine Police Force (PPM) foiled an attempt to smuggle nearly 700,000 lobster fry worth about RM13.4 million to Singapore via sea at Tanjung Belungkor yesterday afternoon. Region Two marine police commander assistant commissioner Noor Azman Jamal said the seizure was carried out by a team from the Johor Marine Intelligence Unit in collaboration with the state Fisheries Department after receiving information on the smuggling activity at about 6.30pm. "Inspection found 124 white polystyrene boxes containing transparent plastic bags filled with an estimated 671,600 lobster fry.

items.title: Rally crowd halts near Jalan TAR–Jalan Tun Perak junction as police form cordon
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The crowd participating in the anti-Azam Baki rally has halted just before the Jalan Tuanku Abdul Rahman–Jalan Tun Perak junction, a short distance from Dataran Merdeka. Among those spotted at the rally were former Economy Minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli; activist Albert Tei; Teoh Lee Lan, sister of the late Teoh Beng Hock; and Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM) deputy chairperson S. Arutchelvan. Police also formed a cordon along Jalan Raja, just before Dataran Merdeka, effectively blocking access to the square as the crowd remained stationed nearby.

items.title: Anwar: Azam Baki performed duties well, now time for new MACC leadership
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim extended his appreciation for outgoing Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Chief Commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki, while voicing confidence in his successor to steer the agency forward. "First, we record our appreciation to Tan Sri Azam Baki, who performed his responsibilities as well as possible. Former High Court Judge Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman has been appointed as the new MACC Chief Commissioner, effective May 13.

items.title: New MACC chief must probe ‘corporate mafia’ claims, says DAP’s Gobind
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — DAP national chairman Gobind Singh Deo today challenged the incoming Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief to investigate allegations of a “corporate mafia” within the agency and resolve the shareholding controversy involving his predecessor, Tan Sri Azam Baki. The call comes after the government announced today the appointment of Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman as the new MACC chief commissioner, effective May 13. Gobind also stressed the need to conclude the investigation into the share ownership controversy involving the outgoing chief, Azam Baki.

items.title: 41 heat-related cases recorded so far - Dzulkefly
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A total of 41 heat-related cases have been recorded so far, with the majority involving heat exhaustion, said Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad. He said that of the total, 35 cases involved heat exhaustion, followed by three cases of heatstroke due to physical activity, two cases of heatstroke indoors, and one case of heat cramps. In addition, he said that Machang district in Kelantan recorded Level 2 hot weather, or a heatwave.

items.title: Penang development allocation up 60pc since 2022, says Anwar
items.summary: BATU KAWAN, April 25 — Development allocation for Penang has increased by up to 60 per cent compared with 2022, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. “So in 2023, Penang was allocated RM5.7 billion, 2024 (RM5.8 billion), 2025 (RM7.6 billion) and for this year, RM7.8 billion. Anwar, who is also the Finance Minister, said among the key projects implemented in Penang were the Light Rail Transit (LRT) Mutiara Line project and the Traffic Dispersal Project between Juru Toll Plaza and Sungai Dua Toll Plaza (PTJSD).

items.title: Iran defence ministry says US seeks 'face-saving' way out of war
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran's defence ministry on Saturday said the US was seeking a "face-saving" way to exit the war, as US envoys travelled to Pakistan for peace negotiations. "Our military power today is a dominant force, and the enemy is looking for a face-saving way to escape the war quagmire it has become trapped in," media outlet ISNA quoted a ministry spokesperson as saying. It comes as emissaries Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner make their way to Islamabad, though Iranian state media has said direct negotiations are not on the cards.

items.title: Gobind, Wee call on new MACC chief to present reforms, restore public trust
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: DAP national chairman Gobind Singh Deo has called on the newly-appointed Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief to outline his vision for the commission. He said Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman must present a clear roadmap of the specific reforms he intends to implement to safeguard the commission's independence and prevent future abuses of power. "His immediate priority must be to restore public confidence in the MACC.

items.title: Govt aims to cut NRW to 28pct under National Water Policy
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH: The implementation of the National Water Policy will focus on key challenges in the water sector, including reducing the high level of non-revenue water (NRW) at the national level. He said the policy encompasses planning at both federal and state levels, while emphasising the overall management of water resources, including distribution and supply system efficiency. "The national water policy involves planning at both the federal and state levels.

items.title: Hybrid work shift boosts demand for co-working spaces
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Co-working operators are preparing to meet Malaysia's shift towards hybrid work arrangements, as demand grows for flexible workspace solutions. Flexible workspace provider WORQ said the trend towards hybrid and flexible work models has moved from a temporary response to a long-term structural shift, particularly in the Klang Valley. Its management said companies are increasingly adopting hybrid arrangements to balance productivity, cost efficiency, employee retention and well-being.

items.title: Over half a million adults suffer from four NCDs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is facing a worrying public health crisis as non-communicable diseases (NCDs) such as diabetes, hypertension, obesity and heart disease continue to pose a major threat to public health. Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said that based on the National Health and Morbidity Survey (NHMS), more than half a million adults in the country suffer from at least four major NCDs simultaneously. He said statistics also show that one in six adults has diabetes, while one in three suffers from high blood pressure.

items.title: Alleged NGO fund misuse highlights need for stronger regulation
items.summary: This matter is deeply troubling as it involves public funds entrusted in good faith to support charitable causes and assist those in need. Incidents of this nature not only tarnish the image of NGOs as a whole but also significantly undermine public confidence in charitable giving. Enhancing transparency and accountability All NGOs that raise public funds must be required to produce regularly audited financial statements and make them accessible to the public to ensure full transparency.

items.title: Azam Baki rally continues after brief fireworks disruption
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Protesters at a rally against Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki resumed a procession towards Dataran Merdeka along Jalan Tuanku Abdul Rahman after a brief disruption. Participants were seen regrouping after the fireworks incident and continuing their march, with some chanting slogans directed at Azam as they moved forward. Police personnel escorted the procession and monitored the situation closely to maintain public order as the crowd moved towards the city centre.

items.title: MRSM bullies identified, says Asyraf Wajdi
items.summary: JASIN: Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) has identified the perpetrators involved in the bullying case at Sultan Azlan Shah Maktab Rendah Sains Mara (MRSM) in Perak, which went viral on social media. Its chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said investigations into the case are in the final stages before stern disciplinary action is taken against those responsible. Asyraf said the victim's family has provided information, and the MRSM management has submitted a formal report on the incident.

items.title: Sabah BN settles leadership line-up under Arthur Joseph Kurup
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 25 — Sabah Barisan Nasional (BN) has finalised all state-level leadership appointments at its first meeting held yesterday, its chairman Datuk Seri Arthur Joseph Kurup said. Under the new line-up, Datuk Jafry Ariffin and Datuk Mohd Hasnol Ayub retained their positions as deputy chairman and Sabah BN secretary, respectively. Also appointed were Datuk Richard Kastum, Datuk Teah Heok Kuin and Chandrra Segar Soosaimany as vice-chairmen, while Datuk Noraini Idris was named Wanita chief, Sufian Abd Karim as Youth chief and Ummi Nabilah Jamal as Puteri chief.

items.title: Show, not just tell: Fahmi wants Madani groups to role-play their way to better public outreach
items.summary: KEPALA BATAS, April 25 — The training format for MADANI Community groups will be enhanced to be more simulation-based to ensure information can be delivered effectively to the public, said Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil. “I have asked the director-general of JaPen (Information Department; Julina Johan) to improve the format of training programmes for the MADANI Community. “MADANI Community groups need to think of unique and critical ways to deliver information.

items.title: Police smash subsidised diesel smuggling syndicate, arrest eight
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police have smashed a subsidised diesel smuggling syndicate with the arrest of eight individuals in Op Taring Bravo 1 at three factories in Shah Alam and Sungai Buloh yesterday. Wildlife Crime Bureau/Intelligence and Special Investigation (WCB/PSK) commander Datuk Mohd Zaki Ashar said 124,950 litres of subsidised diesel worth RM645,632 were seized during the 3.50pm raid. Mohd Zaki said the syndicate, active since early this year, collected subsidised diesel in stages around Selangor before smuggling it out in bulk for sale at RM4 per litre in 5,000-litre quantities to third parties acting as collection and storage centres.

items.title: Loke: Mechanism for diesel road tax reduction proposed
items.summary: BATU KAWAN: The Transport Ministry has proposed a mechanism to the National Economic Action Council (NEAC) secretariat to reduce road tax rates for diesel vehicles to help ease the public burden following rising fuel costs. Its Minister Anthony Loke said the proposal is in line with government efforts to adopt a more comprehensive approach to assist diesel vehicle owners affected by changes in fuel prices. He said diesel prices are currently set on a floating basis to ensure market transparency and curb leakages and smuggling activities.

items.title: RM230mil NGO fund probe: Govt urged to strengthen regulation
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government is urged to take swift measures to strengthen regulation and prevent misconduct among non-governmental organisations (NGOs) following the investigation into the alleged misappropriation of approximately RM230 million involving an NGO, said social activist Tan Sri Lee Lam Thye. "All NGOs that raise public funds must be required to produce regularly audited financial statements and make them accessible to the public to ensure full transparency. Any individual or organisation found guilty of misappropriating public funds must face firm legal action as a deterrent and to uphold justice, Lee said.

items.title: Chaos at anti-Azam Baki rally as fireworks go off [WATCH]
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A brief moment of chaos broke out at a protest rally against Tan Sri Azam Baki today after fireworks were set off near the Sogo shopping complex. The incident, which occurred during a series of speeches, caused the crowd to scatter approximately 50m away from the initial gathering point. © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Azam Baki shareholding probe completed, decision with Chief Secretary
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM: Investigations into allegations involving share ownership linked to Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki have been completed and are awaiting a final decision at the highest level of government. Unity Government spokesman Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the matter was no longer in the investigative stage, with the next step now being handled administratively by the Chief Secretary to the Government, Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar. "The subsequent process is now being managed by the Chief Secretary to the Government.

items.title: Roadblocks set up near LTSIP ahead of motorcycle convoy
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Roadblocks will be carried out this afternoon near Sultan Ismail Petra Airport (LTSIP) in Kota Baru, Kelantan, with the Road Transport Department (RTD) working together with traffic police to inspect all motorcycles entering the airport area. Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the enforcement action follows a planned motorcycle convoy involving several assemblymen to the airport, which he described as irresponsible due to potential disruption to passenger movement and airport operations. "The airport area is a strategic zone, and security must be maintained at all times to ensure smooth and uninterrupted passenger travel.

items.title: Community-run centres bridge education gap in Sabah
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU: In today's modern world, there are often doubts about the quality of education provided by unofficial learning institutions. However, Pacos Trust executive director Anne Lasimbang said the organisation's initiative to establish Community Learning Centres (CLCs) had brought significant changes to children's lives. To date, 25 CLCs have been established across Sabah.

items.title: No compromise: Asyraf Wajdi calls for action over bullying at MRSM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Majlis Amanah Rakyat (Mara) management has been instructed to take firm action over a bullying complaint involving Maktab Rendah Sains Mara (MRSM) Sultan Azlan Shah in Kuala Kangar. Mara chairman Datuk Dr Asyraf Wajdi Dusuki said there would be no compromise on bullying at MRSM. "I have instructed the Mara management to take firm action.

items.title: No permit issued for motorcycle convoy, say Kelantan cops
items.summary: KOTA BARU: Kelantan police have not issued a permit to the organiser of a planned motorcycle convoy scheduled to be held today at several locations, including Sultan Ismail Petra Airport. State police chief Mohd Yusoff Mamat warned that action would be taken against those who proceed with the convoy. Yusoff said he had contacted the organiser last night to explain the regulations governing such events.

items.title: Heavy rain warning: KL, Selangor, and Johor brace for afternoon storms
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning for large swathes of the Klang Valley and southern Peninsular Malaysia, with heavy rain and strong winds expected until late afternoon. The alert, issued at 1.45pm, affects the entirety of Kuala Lumpur and several key districts in Selangor, including Klang, Petaling, and Hulu Langat. Several districts in Pahang and Tawau in Sabah are also included in the alert.

items.title: Palestinians vote in first elections since Gaza war
items.summary: RAMALLAH: Palestinians in the West Bank and a central area of Gaza began voting Saturday in municipal elections in the first vote since the Gaza war, marked by a narrow political field and widespread disillusionment. Nearly 1.5 million people are registered to vote in the Israeli-occupied West Bank, as well as 70,000 people in Gaza's Deir el-Balah area, according to the Ramallah-based Central Elections Commission. AFP footage from Al-Bireh in the West Bank and Deir el-Balah in Gaza showed election officials in polling stations as Palestinians came to cast ballots.

items.title: Rain dampens start of ‘Tangkap Azam Baki’ rally at Sogo, but surprise fireworks briefly cause chaos (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — A planned rally against outgoing Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief Tan Sri Azam Baki started slowly this afternoon, its momentum dampened by a downpour and the government’s surprise announcement this morning that Azam would be replaced. The protest’s turnout comes after the government unexpectedly announced this morning that Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman will be the next MACC chief commissioner. Attendees gather to watch speakers deliver speeches during a rally against Tan Sri Azam Baki, in Kuala Lumpur on April 25, 2026.

items.title: Woman suffers miscarriage, broken arm after alleged assault by husband
items.summary: The victim, who was 12 weeks pregnant, is being treated at a hospital for multiple injuries, including a broken arm and suspected fractured ribs. The victim's brother said the couple married in Thailand last December but had yet to register the union in Malaysia. The man also allegedly fled with the victim's jewellery and cash amounting to nearly RM50,000.

items.title: Transport minister confirms MTEN to study lowering road tax for non-subsidised diesel owners
items.summary: BATU KAWAN, April 25 — The Ministry of Transport has officially submitted a proposal to the National Economic Action Council (MTEN) to study a reduction in road tax for diesel vehicles, Transport Minister Anthony Loke confirmed today. Speaking at the 80th-anniversary celebration of the Road Transport Department (JPJ) here, Loke explained that while diesel prices are now set on a floating basis to curb smuggling, the government is looking for other ways to assist those affected. The proposed road tax reduction is aimed at those who fall outside these categories.

items.title: Sogo area calm ahead of planned Azam Baki rally
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police personnel were seen leaving the Dang Wangi district police headquarters and heading towards the Sogo shopping complex area today, where demonstrators were expected to gather for the Himpunan Tangkap Azam Baki. A check around Jalan Tuanku Abdul Rahman near Sogo, however, found the area remained calm and unusually quiet despite earlier expectations of a crowd forming. Several police personnel were stationed around the area as part of monitoring and control measures to maintain public order and prevent any untoward incidents.

items.title: Loke lambasts Kelantan lawmakers for ‘irresponsible’ Mat Motor convoy through Kota Bharu airport, orders roadblocks
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — Transport Minister Anthony Loke has condemned several Kelantan assemblymen for organising a “Mat Motor” convoy to the Sultan Ismail Petra Airport in Kota Bharu, labelling the act as "highly irresponsible" and a potential threat to public safety and airport operations. The minister said he has directed the Road Transport Department (JPJ) to set up roadblocks in cooperation with traffic police to inspect all motorcycles entering the airport area this evening. In a statement today, Loke questioned the decision to hold a large motorcycle convoy in a strategic and security-sensitive zone like an airport, which must be kept clear to ensure smooth passenger travel.

items.title: MetMalaysia issues thunderstorm warning across several states
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia) has issued a thunderstorm warning, with heavy rain and strong winds expected across six states and a federal territory until 4pm today. Areas expected to be affected include Selangor (Kuala Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling and Hulu Langat) and Kuala Lumpur. In Sabah, the warning covers Tawau.

items.title: Police cripple RM1.8m diesel smuggling syndicate in Shah Alam, Sungai Buloh
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — Police have dismantled a subsidised diesel smuggling syndicate with the arrest of eight individuals in Ops Taring Bravo 1 at three factories in Shah Alam and Sungai Buloh, yesterday. Wildlife Crime Bureau/Intelligence and Special Investigation (WCB/PSK) commander Datuk Mohd Zaki Ashar said 124,950 litres of subsidised diesel worth RM645,632 were seized during the 3.50 pm raid. Mohd Zaki said the syndicate, active since early this year, collected subsidised diesel in stages around Selangor before smuggling it out in bulk for sale at RM4 per litre in 5,000-litre quantities to third parties acting as collection and storage centres.

items.title: Govt to update zakat guidelines to strengthen governance
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government will update the guidelines on the zakat system to further strengthen governance and address emerging concerns, said Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan. He said while existing frameworks and references were already in place, there was room to enhance them to ensure the zakat system remained robust, transparent and responsive. Zulkifli said the government remained confident in the country's zakat institutions, as Malaysia's system had received international recognition and multiple awards.

items.title: PM Anwar urges JPJ to be more professional, firm in enforcement
items.summary: BATU KAWAN, April 25 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has called on the Road Transport Department (JPJ) to continue enhancing its professionalism and firmness in carrying out its duties, particularly in enforcing traffic laws. He said that as an agency that deals directly with the public, every action taken by JPJ is subject to public attention and scrutiny, thus requiring a high level of integrity and efficiency. The Prime Minister said this when speaking at the JPJ Day 80th anniversary celebration and JPJ MADANI Mega Carnival at the State Stadium grounds here today.

items.title: First female Archbishop of Canterbury to meet Pope Leo
items.summary: VATICAN CITY: New Archbishop of Canterbury Sarah Mullally, the first woman to lead the world's Anglican Christians, heads to Rome and the Vatican this weekend for an audience with Pope Leo XIV. Mullally will meet the head of the world's 1.4 billion Catholics during a four-day visit starting Saturday, her first abroad since being enthroned last month as the Church of England's top cleric. The audience comes 60 years after a historic meeting in 1966 between then archbishop Michael Ramsey and pope Paul VI, the first at that level since the Church of England was created in the 16th century, when king Henry VIII broke with Rome.

items.title: Anwar highlights sharp rise in Penang infrastructure spending
items.summary: BATU KAWAN: Federal government development spending in Penang has increased by about 60 per cent under the Madani administration, a move the government said reflects a more balanced and inclusive approach to national development. Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said allocations to the state, which fell below RM5 billion in 2021 and 2022, were increased in the years that followed. Speaking at the Road Transport Department's 80th anniversary celebration here today, Anwar said allocations for Penang rose to RM5.7 billion in 2023, RM5.8 billion in 2024, and RM7.6 billion in 2025, with RM7.8 billion allocated this year.

items.title: New MACC chief Abdul Halim Aman: What do we know?
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, May 13 — After the announcement of Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman as the new Chief Commissioner of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC), Malaysians will likely be curious about Abdul Halim’s background. Abdul Halim brings to his new role decades of judicial experience. A former High Court judge, Abdul Halim retired from the bench in 2023 after nearly two decades of service.

items.title: Anwar: RTD's digital shift not just an upgrade, but a 'necessity' for integrity
items.summary: BATU KAWAN: The Road Transport Department (RTD) must spearhead digital reform as part of broader efforts to strengthen integrity, reduce public friction and meet rising expectations for public service delivery, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said. Anwar said the agency's frontline enforcement role made it one of the most exposed government bodies, where every decision is scrutinised by the public — particularly in the era of social media. Anwar said rising public expectations, amplified by digital connectivity, meant government agencies must adapt quickly and responsibly.

items.title: Anwar floats diesel road tax cut to offset high pump prices
items.summary: BATU KAWAN, April 25 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has instructed the Transport Ministry to study the possibility of reducing road tax for diesel vehicles, offering a potential relief measure for owners grappling with soaring fuel prices that the government says it cannot afford to lower directly. The move comes after diesel prices in Peninsular Malaysia surged dramatically, jumping from around RM3 per litre in late February to over RM5.50 per litre by late March, following adjustments to the government's subsidy mechanism. In Malaysia, road tax for diesel vehicles is generally higher than for petrol vehicles of the same engine capacity due to an additional diesel surcharge.

items.title: Guan Eng backs Selangor DAP rep over pig farming row, accuses PAS of ‘double standards’
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — DAP advisor Lim Guan Eng has responded to critics of Seri Kembangan assemblyman Wong Siew Ki, accusing PAS of applying “double standards” for labelling her “treasonous” over her suggestion to review state pig farming policies. “If PAS is consistent and responsible, they should have taken action against the PAS minister who was reprimanded by the King,” Lim said. He also expressed regret that Umno had joined PAS in criticising Wong.

items.title: Padini says MACC probe targets ‘external parties,’ not company itself
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — Padini Holdings Berhad has clarified that the recent freezing of its bank accounts by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) is related to an investigation involving its “external counterparties,” and not the company or its management. The statement follows Padini’s official filing with Bursa Malaysia yesterday, which first confirmed that several of its bank accounts had been frozen under the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (AMLA). Padini’s statement today specifies that the investigation targets certain external parties who are “not employees, officers, or part of the group’s management.” “The company wishes to emphasise that, based on information currently available, we are not aware of any allegation or wrongdoing on our part and understand that the freezing order is a procedure taken in the course of the investigation,” Padini said.

items.title: Organisers say rally against Azam Baki to proceed despite announcement of new MACC chief
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — The “Tangkap Azam Baki” rally calling for action against outgoing Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief Tan Sri Azam Baki will still proceed this afternoon despite his named replacement, its organisers said today. “The rally will proceed as planned, and our three demands still stand. Earlier today, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar announced that the new MACC chief from May 13 onwards would be Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman.

items.title: Bomba deploys helicopter to water-bomb Dungun peat fire as big as housing estate
items.summary: DUNGUN, April 25 — The Fire and Rescue Department has deployed a helicopter to assist in extinguishing a peat swamp forest fire that has been spreading towards residential areas in Kampung Baru, Kuala Abang, near here since yesterday. Terengganu Fire and Rescue Department assistant director of operations Mohd Izwan Hashim said an Agusta AW139 helicopter from the Northern Territory Air Base in Bertam, Penang, was mobilised to carry out water-bombing operations in areas inaccessible to ground teams. He added that water from fire hydrants is also being used for firefighting operations in residential areas.

items.title: HR Ministry: WFH productivity not a myth, 62pc of bosses agree
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 25 — The implementation of flexible working arrangements (FWA) is in line with the needs of an increasingly dynamic labour market that requires more adaptive, responsive and productivity-driven work models, according to the Ministry of Human Resources (KESUMA). The ministry, in a statement today, said the implementation of FWA – which includes hybrid work models, flexible working hours and arrangements – can enhance organisational efficiency while improving employee well-being. “It also, among others, gives employees the right to apply for flexible working arrangements and requires employers to provide a written response within a stipulated period,” the ministry said.

items.title: Japanese murder fugitive who posed as Myanmar national deported after years on the run
items.summary: The Royal Malaysia Police (PDRM) confirmed the deportation today, completing a complex international investigation that relied on forensic science, including a crucial DNA match, to unmask the wanted man. The man was initially arrested by the Kedah police’s Narcotic Criminal Investigation Department for drug and immigration offences. According to Criminal Investigation Department (CID) Director Commissioner Datuk M. Kumar, investigators used scientific and forensic methods to confirm the fugitive’s true identity.

items.title: Malaysia names new MACC chief ahead of planned rally in KL
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — The Malaysian government today announced a new Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) chief commissioner, pre-empting a planned rally this evening to demand the removal of the incumbent. Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar this morning announced that the new MACC chief from May 13 onwards would be Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman. Shamsul Azri said the King had assented to Abdul Halim’s appointment as the new MACC chief, in line with Section 5(1) of the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Act 2009.

items.title: Anwar in Penang today for JPJ 80th anniversary, Seberang Perai development talks
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 25 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim will undertake a one-day working visit to Penang today to attend several key official programmes focusing on development and public well-being. Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, is scheduled to attend the 80th anniversary celebration of the Road Transport Department (JPJ) and the JPJ MADANI Mega Carnival at the Penang State Stadium grounds in Batu Kawan at 10.30 am. Also accompanying him at the event are Transport Minister Anthony Loke, Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abdul Bakar, and JPJ director-general Datuk Aedy Fadly Ramli.

items.title: Is your meat safe? Police seize over a ton of illegal pork in major Skudai bust
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — Police raided a frozen food distribution outlet in Skudai, Johor, today for allegedly storing pork carcasses without valid slaughter approval. The Internal Security and Public Order Department’s Wildlife Crime Bureau/Special Intelligence Investigation (WCB/PSK) commander, Datuk Mohd Zaki Ashar, said the 10.30 am raid found frozen pork carcasses with dubious slaughter documentation. Mohd Zaki said seven people were detained, including six Nepalese men and a local woman who owns the premises.

items.title: Anwar’s aide: Oil royalties should be used for projects, not political capital
items.summary: IPOH, April 25 — The federal government’s approach toward petroleum royalty payments to state governments should be viewed within the context of development project implementation and should not be used as cheap political capital by the opposition to gain public support. Political Secretary to the Minister of Finance, Muhammad Kamil Abdul Munim, stated that this step is an existing mechanism involving coordination between the federal and state governments to ensure funds are utilised effectively. According to him, while KR1M involved large-scale use of government funds, MADANI Mart is implemented through collaboration between Yayasan MADANI and private parties interested in the retail business.

items.title: Jakim: State religious councils not involved in ‘zakat’ fraud allegations
items.summary: PADANG RENGAS, April 25 — Malaysian Islamic Development Department (Jakim) has urged the public to continue trusting the tithe institution and let authorities investigate recent viral allegations of zakat fund misappropriation. Its director-general, Datuk Dr Sirajuddin Suhaimee, said the State Islamic Religious Councils are not involved in the issue. “The councils entrusted with managing zakat collection are not involved.

items.title: Two-year-old among four riding one motorcycle dies after Bukit Mertajam road crash
items.summary: BUKIT MERTAJAM, April 25 — A two-year-old boy was killed, while his mother was seriously injured, after the motorcycle they were riding with two others skidded and crashed into a stone wall along Jalan Song Ban Kheng in Bukit Mertajam yesterday. She was riding the motorcycle with her three children as passengers. “She sustained serious head injuries, while her two-year-old son was pronounced dead while receiving treatment at Bukit Mertajam Hospital due to head injuries,” he said in a statement last night.

items.title: Selangor MB: 168-acre Permatang Kumbang plot in Puchong hasn’t been a forest reserve since 1926
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 25 — Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari yesterday clarified that a 168-acre plot of land in Permatang Kumbang, Puchong city centre, is not a permanent forest reserve as claimed by irresponsible parties. Amirudin said the land, located near the Ayer Hitam Forest Reserve in Kinrara, was degazetted in 1926, approximately 100 years ago. Amirudin said his administration has never approved any development on the land.

items.title: In Sandakan, some fire survivors say being turned away from full relief centres
items.summary: SANDAKAN, April 25 — Some victims of the Kampung Bahagia fire here are appealing to the authorities to open more relief centres, claiming they were unable to register as existing centres are already full and registration has been closed. One victim, Almahmud Muhamir, 37, said his application was rejected when he tried to register at the relief centre at the Batu Sapi People’s Housing Project Hall yesterday. “When I got there, I was told registration was no longer open.

items.title: Middle finger and road rage: Melaka police search for viral express bus driver
items.summary: MELAKA, April 25 — Police have urged a chartered bus driver, recorded on video allegedly behaving provocatively and making a rude hand gesture towards a car driver Thursday, to come forward to assist in investigations. Melaka Tengah district police chief ACP Christopher Patit said police detected the video at about 2 pm Thursday after it went viral on Facebook, where it was uploaded by an account under the name Muhazam Shah. “The victim, a 61-year-old local man driving a Proton Persona, lodged a police report on the incident at 11.38 am yesterday.

items.title: Two bikers arrested after Putrajaya bridge drag races spark public uproar
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — Two motorcyclists were arrested by the police for allegedly performing dangerous stunts along Putra Bridge in Putrajaya that went viral on Thursday. Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief ACP Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said they identified one out of two rmotorcyclists performing dangerous stunts on high-powered motorcycles before the man, 31, showed up at the Putrajaya police headquarters’ traffic station and confessed to performing the stunts at 12.30 am April 19. The police also arrested another motorcyclist, 33, and seized two high-powered motorcycles believed to be used for the stunts, he said, adding that the case is being investigated under Section 81 of the Road Transport Act 1987.

items.title: Police probe brazen Ipoh cable theft after TikTok video goes viral
items.summary: IPOH, April 25 — Police are tracking down a group of individuals who went viral on social media and are believed to be involved in cable theft at Jalan Raja Musa Aziz here. Ipoh police chief ACP Muhammad Najib Hamzah said the video was uploaded by social activist Faiz under the username @faiz.sosialaktivis on TikTok. Police view this incident seriously as it could affect property, telecommunications services, and public safety,” he said in a statement.

items.title: Padini says some bank accounts frozen by MACC in anti-money laundering probe
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 25 — Several bank accounts belonging to fashion retailer Padini Holdings Berhad and its subsidiaries have been frozen by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) in connection with an anti-money laundering investigation. In a filing with Bursa Malaysia yesterday, the company announced it was notified on April 24 that the freezing order had been issued under Section 44(1) of the Anti-Money Laundering, Anti-Terrorism Financing and Proceeds of Unlawful Activities Act 2001 (AMLA). Operations not materially affected Padini moved quickly to reassure the market, stating that the freeze is not expected to have a material impact on its financial and daily operations.
